Title: Origin and evolution of transporter substrate specificity within the NPF family

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Preferentially transports indole glucosinolates and contributes to retaining/distributing indole glucosinolates between root and shoot; loss reduces root indole glucosinolate levels and enhances rosette accumulation especially in combination with gtr1 gtr2
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly expressed in root phloem companion cells; co-expressed with GTR1 and GTR2 at tissue level in other tissues
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly expressed in root phloem companion cells; co-expressed with GTR1 and GTR2 at tissue level in other tissues
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in Xenopus laevis oocytes; TEVC electrophysiology; time-course uptake assays; substrate competition assays; Arabidopsis knockout and triple-knockout analysis; micro-grafting; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtGTR3 (NPF2.9) was identified as an indole-specific glucosinolate transporter with strong preference for I3M over 4MTB. 2. I3M, but not 4MTB, induced negative currents in GTR3-expressing oocytes, and GTR3 over-accumulated I3M against a concentration gradient. 3. gtr3 mutants had reduced indole glucosinolates in roots, and the gtr1 gtr2 gtr3 triple mutant showed stronger redistribution toward rosettes, supporting a role in root retention/distribution of indole glucosinolates.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also imports nitrate; nitrate in 100-fold excess did not outcompete glucosinolate uptake, indicating substrates are not mutually exclusive. Apparent Km toward I3M was <25 µM. Originally annotated as NRT1.9-2; hereafter GTR3.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Hydroponic growth; micro-grafting; Xenopus oocyte assays at external pH 5
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Public translatome dataset GSE14493 used for expression context

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Broad-specific glucosinolate transporter that imports both aliphatic and indole glucosinolates and contributes with GTR2/GTR3 to root-shoot distribution of indole glucosinolates
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed with GTR2 and GTR3 at tissue level
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Co-expressed with GTR2 and GTR3 at tissue level
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Xenopus oocyte uptake assays; TEVC electrophysiology; competition assays; Arabidopsis mutant and grafting comparisons
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtGTR1 transported both 4MTB and I3M with similar efficiency and over-accumulated both against a concentration gradient. 2. Both substrates induced electrogenic negative currents. 3. Together with GTR2 and GTR3, GTR1 contributes to indole glucosinolate partitioning between roots and rosettes.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: High apparent affinity toward I3M (<25 µM). Used as broad-specific reference transporter throughout the study.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Xenopus oocyte assays at pH 5; Arabidopsis hydroponic and grafting experiments
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: GSE14493 used for tissue expression context

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Broad-specific glucosinolate transporter contributing with GTR1/GTR3 to glucosinolate distribution between root and shoot
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed with GTR1 and GTR3 at tissue level
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Co-expressed with GTR1 and GTR3 at tissue level
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Xenopus oocyte uptake/electrophysiology; Arabidopsis double- and triple-mutant comparisons
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtGTR2 showed high apparent affinity for I3M (<25 µM). 2. Together with GTR1 it represents a broad-specific glucosinolate transporter. 3. Genetic data from double and triple mutants support participation in indole glucosinolate partitioning between roots and shoots.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously characterized broad-specific glucosinolate transporter; included here mainly as comparator and in genetic combinations.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Xenopus oocyte assays at pH 5; Arabidopsis hydroponic and grafting experiments
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: GSE14493 used for tissue expression context

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Shows strong preference for indole glucosinolate I3M, indicating indole-specific transport is conserved in the GTR3 subclade
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously localized to plasma membrane in Xenopus oocytes
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Codon-optimized heterologous expression in Xenopus oocytes; LC-MS uptake assays; TEVC electrophysiology; YFP localization
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BrGTR3 preferentially transported I3M over 4MTB. 2. Only I3M induced detectable negative currents. 3. This supports conservation of indole preference within the Brassicales-specific GTR3 subclade.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used for evolutionary comparison of glucosinolate transport specificity.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Broad-specific glucosinolate transporter transporting 4MTB and I3M with similar efficiency
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously localized to plasma membrane in Xenopus oocytes
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Codon-optimized heterologous expression in Xenopus oocytes; LC-MS uptake assays; TEVC electrophysiology; YFP localization
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BrGTR1 transported both 4MTB and I3M with similar efficiency. 2. Both substrates induced negative currents. 3. This supports conservation of broad glucosinolate specificity in the GTR1 lineage.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as evolutionary comparator for broad-specific glucosinolate transporters.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Broad-specific glucosinolate transporter from basal glucosinolate-producing species, supporting evolution of high-affinity glucosinolate transport before Brassicaceae diversification
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Localized to plasma membrane in Xenopus oocytes
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Xenopus oocyte uptake assays; TEVC electrophysiology; YFP localization
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CpGTRL1 over-accumulated both 4MTB and I3M. 2. Exposure to both glucosinolates induced currents. 3. It represents a basal broad-specific glucosinolate transporter in the GTR-like clade.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Named CARICA PAPAYA Glucosinolate Transporter LIKE-1.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Broad-specific, high-affinity glucosinolate transporter that transports both 4MTB and I3M
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Localized to plasma membrane in Xenopus oocytes
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Xenopus oocyte uptake assays; TEVC electrophysiology; kinetic analysis; YFP localization
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CpGTRL2 over-accumulated both 4MTB and I3M. 2. It transported 4MTB with an apparent Km of 85 ± 12 µM at -60 mV. 3. Its activity supports that high-affinity broad glucosinolate transport evolved before Brassicaceae-Caricaceae diversification.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Named CARICA PAPAYA Glucosinolate Transporter LIKE-2; both glucosinolates induced currents.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Dual-specific transporter that transports both glucosinolates and the cyanogenic glucoside prunasin, proposed as an evolutionary transition transporter between cyanogenic glucoside and glucosinolate transporters
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Localized to plasma membrane in Xenopus oocytes
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Xenopus oocyte uptake assays; TEVC electrophysiology; phylogenetic analysis; YFP localization
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Me14G074000 over-accumulated both 4MTB and I3M even though cassava does not synthesize glucosinolates. 2. It also accumulated prunasin, but not linamarin, suggesting partial retention of cyanogenic glucoside specificity. 3. No detectable glucosinolate- or cyanogenic glucoside-induced currents were observed, consistent with a non-electrogenic transport mechanism and a proposed transition state in evolution.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper repeatedly proposes this transporter as a 'transition' transporter. Transport may be non-electrogenic or below detection limit. Source species is cassava, but metabolic effects were assayed in Xenopus oocytes.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Inferior transport properties for cyanogenic glucosides compared with MeCGTR1

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: High-affinity cyanogenic glucoside-specific importer that strongly over-accumulates prunasin and linamarin and is proposed to represent the ancestral state for evolution of glucosinolate transporters
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Localized to plasma membrane in Xenopus oocytes
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Xenopus oocyte uptake assays; TEVC electrophysiology; kinetic analysis; phylogenetic comparison; YFP localization
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Me15G180400 specifically transported cyanogenic glucosides, strongly over-accumulating prunasin and linamarin while showing negligible glucosinolate uptake. 2. Prunasin and linamarin induced negative currents in MeCGTR1-expressing oocytes. 3. Kinetic analysis showed high affinity with Km values of 80 ± 7 µM for prunasin and 262 ± 15 µM for linamarin, identifying it as the first importer of cyanogenic glucosides reported by the authors.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Named MANIHOT ESCULENTA CYANOGENIC GLUCOSIDE TRANSPORTER-1 (MeCGTR1). Proposed agro-biotech value for engineering cyanogenic glucoside content in edible crop parts.
